title: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole
summary: CME00058 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic selection from the 1921 revised EETS reissue of George G. Perry's English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole. The encoding retains the revised title page and all thirteen numbered texts on printed pp. 1-48, but omits the prefatory note, Mabel Day's notes and revised glossary, advertisements, and the separately distributed Officium brochure. The 1921 text is a materially changed version of Perry's 1866 first edition: it splits the two contrition narratives, adds the enclosed-maiden exemplum and two Marian prayers, and renumbers the collection. Lincoln Cathedral MS 91 supplies the principal, discontinuous manuscript portions; Royal MS 17 C XVIII supplies the lost opening of Walter Hilton's Mixed Life; Harley MS 1022 supplies variants for Oleum effusum and the two Marian prayers. Complete scans of two 1921 bound copies and two 1866 copies are cached. No publicly obtainable whole-object facsimile was verified for Lincoln MS 91, Harley MS 1022, Royal MS 17 C XVIII, or Cambridge Dd.5.64, so complete-object reproduction routes and exact work loci are recorded separately.
review_notes: This record treats the 1921 revised edited selection, its 1866 prior version, the component manuscripts, scans, U-M encoding, and repository XML as distinct layers.

The title-page attribution to Richard Rolle is historical edition framing, not a reliable authorship statement for every component. Current cataloging identifies Walter Hilton's Of Angels' Song and Epistle on the Mixed Life, while the final prayers are anonymous.

A complete cached facsimile means the full provider-exposed physical book or manuscript object was retained, including covers, blanks, adjacent works, and later matter when exposed. The corpus work is mapped inside that object only through local_copies[].work_portion.

The BSB IIIF metadata dates its object to 1921, but direct inspection of its internal wrapper, title page, preface, ten-item numbering, and imprint establishes that the imaged edition is Perry's 1866 first edition. Both assertions are preserved.
Perry's 1921 headnote prints Harl. MS 1002 for the Marian prayers; the British Library catalog and DIMEV identify the witness as Harley MS 1022, fol. 61v.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00058
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow.
notes: The pinned XML is 179,100 bytes.
Its TEMPHEAD records a 2016-09-09 structural and proofing review, not the date of original keying.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00058
entities.description: A digital edition-level selection containing the 1921 revised title page and numbered texts I-XIII on printed pp. 1-48. It omits the prefatory note, Notes pp. 49-50, Glossary and Index pp. 51-55, the companion Officium brochure, advertisements, and other bound matter.

date_statements.label: repository XML review
date_statements.notes: A review date for this serialization, not a claimed first-publication date.

entities.label: Perry and Furnivall, English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole, revised reissue (1921)
alternate_labels: A new and revised Text and Glossary
entities.description: The immediate print source for CME00058: a 1921 reissue of EETS Original Series 20 with a revised text prepared by Frederick J. Furnivall, a glossary revised by Mabel Day, and notes added by Day. The selection prints thirteen numbered items on pp. 1-48.

bibliographic.citation: George G. Perry, ed., English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole, revised text by Frederick J. Furnivall, glossary revised and notes added by Mabel Day (London: published for the Early English Text Society by Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, 1921), EETS Original Series 20.
bibliographic.title: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 20
bibliographic.publisher: Published for the Early English Text Society by Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press
bibliographic.pages: Prefatory note; text pp. 1-48; Notes pp. 49-50; Glossary and Index pp. 51-55

notes: The title page retains the dates '1866, 1921'; the prefatory note calls this a reissue and identifies the revised-text, glossary, and notes responsibilities.
CME00058 encodes the title page and the complete revised text pp. 1-48, but none of the prefatory note, Notes, Glossary and Index, advertisements, or companion brochure.

entities.label: George G. Perry, English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole (1866)
entities.description: Perry's first edition and the prior version of the 1921 text. It has ten numbered items: the two contrition narratives are one item, the enclosed-maiden exemplum and Marian prayers are absent, and the later items therefore have different numbers.

bibliographic.citation: George G. Perry, ed., English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole: Who Died A.D. 1349 (London: published for the Early English Text Society by N. Trübner & Co., 1866), EETS Original Series 20.
bibliographic.title: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole: Who Died A.D. 1349
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 20
bibliographic.publisher: Published for the Early English Text Society by N. Trübner & Co.

notes: The text occupies pp. 1-45 and the glossary pp. 46-49.
Its scope corresponds to revised items I-V and VII-XII, with revised III-IV combined as one 1866 item. Revised items VI and XIII do not occur.

entities.label: Lincoln Cathedral Library MS 91
entities.description: Robert Thornton's c. 1430-1450 paper miscellany and the principal manuscript source for Perry's selection. The selected texts occupy discontinuous loci from ff. 192r-230v amid extensive adjacent devotional, literary, and medical material.

holding.notes: Relevant loci are ff. 192r-196v, 219v-221v, 223r-230v, with substantial non-selected matter between them.

physical_description.extent: 322 numbered leaves, plus six paper leaves at front and three at back, from an original minimum of 340
physical_description.format: Composite literary, devotional, and medical codex in Robert Thornton's hand; current 1975 S. R. Cockerell binding
notes: Current cataloging records damaged or missing matter and several fragments.
The selected Rolle/Hilton material is only a small, discontinuous portion of the whole codex.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1022
entities.description: A Northern English religious miscellany of c. 1390-c. 1425. Perry collated its Oleum effusum at ff. 62r-64r and the 1921 revision prints the two Marian prayers from f. 61v.

holding.notes: The prayers are at f. 61v and Oleum effusum at ff. 62r-64r.

physical_description.extent: ff. 97 + 15* + 16*, plus five unfoliated modern front flyleaves, four rear flyleaves, a paper stub, and a pastedown
physical_description.format: Religious prose-and-verse miscellany in a post-1600 British Museum binding
notes: The British Library catalog localizes the manuscript to Northern England.
A complete-object surrogate must retain every flyleaf, pastedown, stub, blank, numbered leaf, cover or board, spine, and adjacent work.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 17 C XVIII
entities.description: A fifteenth-century vellum theological miscellany. Its incomplete copy of Walter Hilton's Epistle on the Mixed Life begins at f. 57 and supplies the opening of Perry's item XI through printed p. 27 line 29, after which Perry changes to the incomplete Lincoln witness.

holding.notes: The Mixed Life begins at f. 57 and the following cataloged item begins at f. 65, bounding the component at approximately ff. 57r-64v. Perry does not identify the exact folio at which his supplied opening ends.

notes: The current catalog requires a letter of introduction for consultation.
A complete-object surrogate must retain every exterior and interior surface and all adjacent treatises, not only ff. 57r-64v.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Dd.5.64
entities.description: A late-fourteenth-century manuscript of works associated with Richard Rolle. Mabel Day's 1921 Notes explicitly cite Dd. v. 64 for readings on printed p. 13 and use 'MS. Camb.' repeatedly on pp. 17-20. Whether every generic Cambridge citation refers to Dd.5.64 requires confirmation.

holding.notes: The exact medieval-manuscript catalog entry was not available as a current item-level web record; CUL states that most medieval manuscript descriptions remain in its printed catalogs.

entities.label: Unidentified Cambridge University Library witness of Walter Hilton's Mixed Life
entities.description: The Cambridge witness compared in Perry's item XI headnote. Perry says that it is in a later dialect than both the Lincoln and Royal copies and prints one comparative sentence, but supplies no shelfmark. It is not silently equated with Dd.5.64.

holding.notes: Perry's 1866 and 1921 text gives only 'Cambridge University Library.'

entities.label: Internet Archive Boston College complete scan of the 1921 revised reissue
entities.description: An 80-page PDF retaining the cover, blanks, series leaves, title, prefatory note, complete text, Notes, Glossary and Index, advertisements, and rear matter of one Boston College copy.

entities.label: Internet Archive University of Toronto complete bound scan of the 1921 revised reissue
entities.description: A 114-page PDF retaining the complete revised reissue, later EETS advertisements, and a separately bound companion Officium/Legenda brochure, together with covers, blanks, and end matter.

entities.label: Wikimedia Commons and Internet Archive complete scan of Perry's 1866 first edition
entities.description: An 85-page PDF retaining library leaves, the title, thirty-three-page preface, complete ten-item text, and glossary of a Catholic University of America copy.

entities.label: BSB/MDZ complete IIIF object for Perry's 1866 first edition
entities.description: A 90-canvas complete digital book object. Although provider metadata says 1921, its imaged wrapper, title, preface, ten-item structure, and imprint identify the 1866 first edition.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with 90 JPEG page images

entities.label: Lincoln Cathedral catalog selected image for MS 91
entities.description: The one unlabeled 1071 x 698 color JPEG linked from the MS 91 catalog. The record supplies no folio label, so it is not assigned a CME00058 work_portion.

entities.label: The Thornton Manuscript (Lincoln Cathedral MS 91), Scolar Press facsimile
entities.description: A printed whole-manuscript photographic facsimile with introductions by D. S. Brewer and A. E. B. Owen. No openly downloadable complete copy was verified.

bibliographic.citation: The Thornton Manuscript (Lincoln Cathedral MS 91), introductions by D. S. Brewer and A. E. B. Owen (London: Scolar Press, 1975).
bibliographic.title: The Thornton Manuscript (Lincoln Cathedral MS 91)

entities.label: Digital Index of Middle English Verse version 1.0
entities.description: A downloadable scholarly dataset describing the two Marian prayers as DIMEV records 4432/NIMEV 2792 and 3437/NIMEV 2123 and locating both in Harley MS 1022, f. 61v.
notes: DIMEV's bibliography labels Perry pp. 47-48 as Perry1866 even though direct edition comparison shows the prayers occur only in the 1921 revision; that bibliographic mismatch is not used to move the prayers into the 1866 edition.

entities.label: British Library Digitisation and Imaging Services
entities.description: The current request, price, delivery, color-photography, and permissions route for complete-object surrogates of Harley MS 1022 and Royal MS 17 C XVIII.

agents.name: George Gresley Perry
notes: Editor of the 1866 first edition; the 1921 reissue retains his edition title and base selection.

agents.name: Frederick James Furnivall
notes: Prepared the revised text printed in the 1921 reissue.

agents.name: Mabel Day
notes: Revised Furnivall's unfinished glossary and added the 1921 Notes.

agents.name: Richard Rolle
notes: Author or traditional attributed author of several selected texts; not author of the entire edited selection.

agents.name: Walter Hilton
notes: Modern cataloging identifies Of Angels' Song and the Epistle on the Mixed Life as Hilton's.

agents.name: Robert Thornton
notes: Scribe and compiler of Lincoln Cathedral MS 91, not author of the selected texts.

agents.name: Digital Index of Middle English Verse

subject.description: Encoded title page and numbered items I-XIII

object.description: 1921 title page and revised text only

scope.notes: The prefatory note, Notes pp. 49-50, Glossary and Index pp. 51-55, companion Officium brochure, advertisements, and other bound matter are omitted.

subject.description: Revised thirteen-item text, revised glossary, new Notes, and reissue framing

scope.notes: The 1921 text splits the two contrition narratives into revised III-IV, adds the enclosed-maiden exemplum as VI and two Marian prayers as XIII, and renumbers later items. It is not a page-for-page reprint.

subject.description: The Lincoln-derived portions of the 1866 ten-item text
locators: item IX after its Royal-supplied opening through p. 41

object.description: Discontinuous selected texts in the Thornton manuscript
locators: Of the Virtues of the Holy Name, ff. 192r-193v
The Bee and the Stork, ff. 194r-v
Seven Gifts of the Holy Ghost, ff. 196r-v
Mixed Life after the supplied opening, ff. 223r-229r or 229v

scope.notes: Perry's first edition omits the enclosed-maiden text at ff. 194v-195r and the Marian prayers in Harley MS 1022.

subject.description: Supplied opening of item IX, Walter Hilton's Mixed Life
locators: equivalent 1921 printed p. 21 through p. 27 line 29

object.description: Opening of the incomplete Royal copy of Mixed Life
locators: component begins f. 57r and ends before the next item at f. 65r

scope.notes: Perry explicitly marks the point in the printed text at which he changes from Royal to Lincoln, but not the corresponding Royal folio.

subject.description: Item I text and footnote variants
Item IX editorial headnote and comparison sentence

object.description: Unidentified Cambridge copy of Mixed Life

scope.notes: Perry characterizes the dialect and prints one comparative sentence but does not identify a shelfmark.

subject.description: Added item VI, De vita cuiusdam puelle incluse

scope.notes: This text is absent from the 1866 first edition and therefore requires a direct revised-edition relation rather than inheritance through version_of alone.

subject.description: Added item XIII, two Marian prayers

object.description: Quene of parage and Mary so milde

scope.notes: The printed headnote's shelfmark 1002 is corrected to Harley MS 1022 from the current British Library catalog and DIMEV.

subject.description: Mabel Day's revised-edition Notes citing a Cambridge witness
locators: Notes pp. 49-50, entries for text pp. 13 and 17-20

object.description: Dd.5.64 readings cited explicitly or likely under the abbreviated label MS. Camb.

scope.notes: Only the p. 13 entry spells out Dd. v. 64, but the p. 17-20 'MS. Camb.' readings occur in the same contiguous correction list. Their identity with Dd.5.64 is therefore a high-confidence contextual inference, still awaiting folio-level confirmation.

object.description: Complete main 1921 revised reissue with its bound advertisements

subject.description: Main revised reissue within a larger bound object

scope.notes: The remaining PDF pages include a separately distributed companion Officium/Legenda brochure and additional end matter.
Identification follows the photographed book rather than the conflicting 1921 provider date.
No work_portion is assigned because neither the folio nor target-text overlap is established.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A complete 1921 revised EETS reissue with preliminary, textual, explanatory, lexical, advertising, and companion matter
editorial_practices.representation: Title page plus numbered texts I-XIII only
editorial_practices.description: The encoding includes the revised title page and printed pp. 1-48 while omitting the prefatory note, Notes pp. 49-50, Glossary and Index pp. 51-55, advertisements, and the companion Officium brochure.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The Lincoln copy of Mixed Life is imperfect at the beginning
editorial_practices.representation: Royal MS 17 C XVIII supplies the opening through the later revised p. 27 line 29; the text then changes to Lincoln MS 91
editorial_practices.description: Perry prints a composite Mixed Life rather than a homogeneous transcription of one manuscript and describes the dialect change in the headnote.
editorial_practices.scope: 1866 item IX and inherited 1921 item XI

editorial_practices.source_feature: A second copy of Oleum effusum in Harley MS 1022
editorial_practices.representation: Harley variants printed as footnotes against the Lincoln text
editorial_practices.description: Perry explicitly identifies Harley MS 1022, fol. 62, as the source of item I footnote readings and notes its initial qw against Thornton's wh.

editorial_practices.representation: Many editorially supplied letters inside words are distinguished in italic type and encoded as HI REND=italic
editorial_practices.description: The 1921 print and XML systematically use mid-word italics in forms such as Ihesu, with, per, and omitted nasal letters. Without complete manuscript images, those italics document editorial expansion choices but do not reconstruct the original abbreviation marks or letterforms.
notes: A complete codex facsimile is needed to compare each typographic expansion with the manuscript's exact sign.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Latin text written in red in Lincoln MS 91
editorial_practices.representation: Editorial marginal notes state 'Latin in red'; the XML preserves the wording but not manuscript color
editorial_practices.description: Color information survives as editorial prose rather than as a color facsimile or consistently structured markup.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Perry's ten-item 1866 text and additional manuscript material
editorial_practices.representation: Thirteen renumbered items in a revised, reset text
editorial_practices.description: The revised text separates Perry's combined contrition narratives, inserts the enclosed-maiden exemplum, adds two Harley Marian prayers, and renumbers the remaining items. The prefatory note confirms Furnivall prepared the revised text but does not fully document his copy-text or emendation method.

local_copies.label: Complete 90-canvas BSB IIIF bundle for the 1866 first edition

work_portion.label: Prior-version title and text corresponding to part of the 1921 CME00058 selection
locators: 1866 ten-item text, printed pp. 1-45: IIIF canvases 37-81
notes: Revised item VI and item XIII are absent; revised items III-IV are one 1866 item.
The bundle includes every canvas and preserves the provider manifest and inventory.

The requested derivative intent was width 1800 pixels, but every provider image resolved to the manifest's native full URL. The inventory records image_request_size=full for all 90 canvases; sampled images were approximately 1499-1550 pixels wide, so the bundle is not described as uniformly 1800 pixels.

access.format: 90-canvas color IIIF object; cached auditable ZIP

The current price list is per page/image and a whole-object order is likely to require an individual quote and substantial cost.

For whole-object evidence, request covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every manuscript leaf, and all adjacent works; state the target passages separately rather than ordering only those passages.
Request publication or repository-use permission separately from image supply if reuse is contemplated.

rights.component: The medieval textual content selected from ff. 192r-230v
rights.basis: The component texts and surviving manuscript copy are medieval, and their selected readings were published in 1866 or 1921; U.S. Copyright Office guidance states that works published before 1931 are in the U.S. public domain.
notes: This status does not determine rights or contractual conditions in modern manuscript photography, catalog text, or supplied digital files.

rights.component: The medieval Oleum effusum and Marian-prayer texts at ff. 61v-64r
rights.basis: The component texts and manuscript copy are medieval and the selected readings were published no later than 1921; U.S. Copyright Office guidance states that works published before 1931 are in the U.S. public domain.
notes: Modern photographs and supplied-image terms are separate layers.

rights.component: The fifteenth-century copy of Walter Hilton's Mixed Life at approximately ff. 57r-64v
rights.basis: The component text and manuscript copy are medieval and the selected reading was published in 1866; U.S. Copyright Office guidance states that works published before 1931 are in the U.S. public domain.

rights.component: Medieval textual readings cited by the 1921 Notes
rights.basis: The manuscript and cited textual readings are medieval and were published in sources before 1931.

rights.component: Perry's 1866 edition, preface, transcription, annotations, and glossary
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1866; current U.S. Copyright Office guidance states that works published before 1931 are in the public domain in the United States.

rights.component: The 1921 revised text, prefatory note, Notes, revised glossary, and other edition-level contribution
rights.basis: The revised reissue was published in 1921; current U.S. Copyright Office guidance states that works published before 1931 are in the public domain in the United States.
notes: This U.S. status does not by itself authorize reuse of separately protected provider photography or override access contracts outside the United States.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M item CME00058, including the electronic transcription and interface text
rights.basis: The item page states that the materials are in the public domain and gives the DPLA No Copyright - United States rights statement; the collection page explicitly qualifies the public-domain assertion to the United States.

rights.component: Boston College/Internet Archive page-image PDF and associated scan metadata
rights.basis: The source book is a U.S.-public-domain 1921 publication; Internet Archive metadata reports no copyright page and exposes the files publicly, but provides no item-specific open license for the modern scan files.
notes: Public download is recorded independently from redistribution permission.

rights.component: University of Toronto/Internet Archive page-image PDF and associated scan files
rights.basis: Internet Archive metadata records the University of Toronto scanning center's item-level status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the United States, based on the 1921 date and absence of a visible notice.

rights.component: Wikimedia Commons PDF file and the 1866 edition it reproduces
rights.basis: The exact Commons file record marks the file public domain, copyrighted=false, and attribution-required=false.

rights.component: BSB/MDZ IIIF manifest, page images, and cached complete bundle
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest applies RightsStatements.org No Copyright - Non-Commercial Use Only 1.0 to the digital object.
notes: The historical edition is public domain in the United States, but the provider's explicit noncommercial-use statement governs this access route and must not be replaced by the edition-level status.

rights.basis: Lincoln Cathedral's website terms reserve site-material rights, permit downloading extracts for personal reference, prohibit separate use of photographs from accompanying context, and require a license for commercial use.
notes: The ignored cache is a narrow private research copy, not a redistributable corpus asset.

rights.basis: The library offers charged digital images on request, but no item-level terms for a not-yet-supplied complete object were available.

rights.component: Prospective British Library-supplied complete-object photography of Harley MS 1022
rights.basis: The January 2026 Imaging Services terms state that supplied material may carry intellectual-property rights and must not be copied or reproduced without British Library written permission.
notes: A paid reference copy may be cached privately only under the supplied terms; repository publication, redistribution, and print-on-demand use require separately documented permission.

rights.component: Prospective British Library-supplied complete-object photography of Royal MS 17 C XVIII
rights.basis: The January 2026 Imaging Services terms state that supplied material may carry intellectual-property rights and must not be copied or reproduced without British Library written permission.

rights.component: Prospective Cambridge University Library-supplied complete-object photography
rights.jurisdiction: Cambridge University Library supply and licensing terms
rights.basis: CUL distinguishes private-study image supply from academic or commercial use and requires a use-specific imaging/licensing request for publication or other reuse.

rights.component: Brewer and Owen's 1975 introduction, facsimile publication design, and photographic reproduction
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The facsimile was published in 1975 and no open license was identified in the available catalog record.

rights.component: DIMEV version 1.0 data files, documentation, and repository content
rights.basis: The release LICENSE, README, and Zenodo record apply Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: British Library Digitisation Services webpage text
rights.basis: The British Library page footer states that site text is available under CC BY except where otherwise stated.
notes: This page-text license does not apply to manuscript images ordered through Imaging Services.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME source XML, CME00058.xml.
evidence.summary: Primary repository evidence for identifiers, title-page transcription, thirteen-item structure, printed page milestones, manuscript-locus notes, editorial notes, italic markup, and the 2016 structural-review statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, CME00058 item information and full text.
evidence.summary: Exact provider evidence for the item title, 1921 publication description, structured contents I-XIII, stable identifier, public access, item-level public-domain statement, and No Copyright - United States URI.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection page.
evidence.summary: Provider evidence that the collection was manually keyed from public-domain editions with in-house proofreading, uses basic TEI semantics, offers bulk XML, and applies its public-domain assertion specifically in the United States.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, englishprosetrea00roll, Boston College copy of the 1921 revised reissue.
evidence.locator: PDF pages 1-80; title page 9; text pp. 1-48 at PDF pages 13-60; Notes at 61-62; Glossary and Index at 63-67
evidence.summary: Direct, visually inspected evidence for the complete main 1921 book, exact work mapping, title-page responsibilities, textual wording, page ranges, item numbering, omitted matter, and bound-object completeness.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, englishprosetrea00rolluoft, University of Toronto bound copy of the 1921 revised reissue.
evidence.locator: PDF pages 1-114; title page 9; text pp. 1-48 at PDF pages 13-60; companion Officium/Legenda brochure at PDF pages 77-107
evidence.summary: Direct, visually inspected evidence for a complete larger bound object, including covers, the main reissue, advertisements, a separately distributed companion brochure, blanks and end matter; its metadata asserts NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT in the United States.

evidence.citation: Prefatory Note to English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole (1921).
evidence.locator: PDF page 11, unnumbered Prefatory Note dated 28 November 1921
evidence.summary: Primary edition evidence that Furnivall prepared the revised text, Day revised the unfinished glossary and added the Notes, the obsolete 1866 preface was not reprinted, and the already-bound revised Officium was distributed as a separate brochure.

evidence.citation: Mabel Day's Notes to English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole (1921).
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for proposed emendations and citations to Horstmann, Cambridge Dd. v. 64, generic MS. Camb., the Vernon manuscript, Harley MS 2254, Royal MS 17 C XVIII, and Notary's 1507 print; these Notes are omitted from CME00058.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons and Internet Archive, cuaenglishproset00roll, complete scan of Perry's 1866 first edition.
evidence.locator: PDF pages 1-85; title 5; preface 7-35; text pp. 1-45 at PDF pages 37-81; glossary pp. 46-49 at PDF pages 82-85
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the first edition's 1866 imprint, ten-item structure, title and page mapping, full text, glossary, source headnotes, and differences from the 1921 revised selection.

evidence.citation: George G. Perry, Preface to English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole (1866).
evidence.locator: Preface pp. i-xxxiii, PDF pages 7-35; especially pp. v-vii
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for Perry's claim to first publication, his use of Robert Thornton's miscellaneous manuscript, his historical rationale for Rolle attribution, the Harley collation, the Royal and unidentified Cambridge Mixed Life witnesses, and his editorial limitations.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek/MDZ, bsb10748408, complete 90-canvas IIIF object.
evidence.locator: All 90 canvases; title canvas 5; text pp. 1-45 at canvases 37-81
evidence.summary: Audited complete provider-manifest bundle with 90 of 90 source images and an exact URL/checksum inventory. Direct inspection identifies the photographed book as the 1866 first edition despite provider metadata saying 1921.

evidence.citation: BSB IIIF Presentation manifest for bsb10748408.
evidence.summary: Exact item-level provider evidence for 90 canvases, BSB attribution, provider metadata including the conflicting 1921 date, and the No Copyright - Non-Commercial Use Only 1.0 rights URI.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons exact file record for the cuaenglishproset00roll PDF.
evidence.summary: Exact downstream-provider evidence labeling the file public domain with no copyright and no attribution requirement.

evidence.citation: University of Lincoln Special Collections catalog, Lincoln Cathedral MS 91, The 'Thornton' romances.
evidence.summary: Holding-repository evidence for shelfmarks, c. 1430-1450 date, Robert Thornton provenance, selected component loci, adjoining contents, bibliography, and the one selected image.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Lincoln Cathedral Library MS 91 handlist.
evidence.summary: Current specialist handlist supporting paper material, North Yorkshire association, dimensions, relevant loci, modern identifications of Of Angels' Song and Mixed Life as Walter Hilton's, identification of the f. 229v-230v text as from Hilton's Scale of Perfection, and the Scolar Press facsimile.

evidence.citation: Visual and technical inspection of the selected MS 91 JPEG linked from the Lincoln catalog.
evidence.summary: Confirms one 1071 x 698 color manuscript-page photograph. The catalog supplies no folio label, so the image remains partial and unmapped to CME00058.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Cathedral, Terms of Service Agreement, Intellectual Property Rights.
evidence.summary: Provider terms reserve site-material rights, permit a personal-reference copy or extract, prohibit separate use of photographs from accompanying text, and require a license for commercial use.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Cathedral, Readers and Researchers.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for reader access and charged digital-image requests; it does not promise complete-object availability or redistribution permission.

evidence.citation: Dated complete-digital-object access check for Lincoln Cathedral MS 91.
evidence.summary: The exact current and legacy catalogs expose one unlabeled image and no whole-object IIIF or download. The specialist handlist and Wellcome record identify printed facsimiles only; exact-shelfmark checks found no publicly obtainable complete digital codex. This is a dated negative.

evidence.citation: Wellcome Collection catalog, The Thornton Manuscript (Lincoln Cathedral MS 91).
evidence.summary: Bibliographic evidence for the 1975 Scolar Press printed facsimile, its editors, publication details, physical extent, and current physical-record access.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 1022.
evidence.summary: Exact holding-repository evidence for the shelfmark, c. 1390-c. 1425 date, Northern English origin, material, dimensions, complete foliation and extra surfaces, blank Digitised Content field, two Marian prayers at f. 61v, and Rolle's Oleum effusum at ff. 62r-64r.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Royal MS 17 C XVIII.
evidence.summary: Exact holding-repository evidence for the shelfmark, fifteenth-century date, vellum support, 133-folio extent, dimensions, empty Digitised Content field, viewing restriction, and Walter Hilton's incomplete Mixed Life beginning at f. 57 before the next item at f. 65.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the online order form, required shelfmark and folio information, reference versus reproduction formats, manuscript photography rather than scanning, electronic delivery, explicit color requests, publication-permission route, and CC BY licensing of page text.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: Official terms for preservation review, usual thirty-working-day processing after payment, special handling of large orders, changing prices, third-party rights, and the prohibition on copying or reproducing supplied material without British Library written permission.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation price list 2025-26.
evidence.summary: Current cached fee schedule showing per-image reference and reproduction rates and supporting the conclusion that a full 97- or 133-folio manuscript order requires a substantial quote.

evidence.citation: Dated complete-digital-object checks for Harley MS 1022 and Royal MS 17 C XVIII.
evidence.summary: Both exact catalog records have empty Digitised Content fields, and exact-shelfmark searches found no publicly obtainable complete digital objects. This is a dated negative and does not establish that internal photography or microfilm is absent.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Catalogues and Search the Collections guidance for medieval manuscripts.
evidence.summary: Official evidence that most CUL medieval manuscripts remain described only in printed catalogs and that absence from ArchiveSearch or iDiscover cannot resolve a manuscript's identity or availability.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library Cultural Heritage Imaging Laboratory, Image Supply and Reproduction.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for professional special-collections photography, quote and payment workflow, separate academic/commercial permission requests, and use-specific licensing distinct from underlying rights.

evidence.citation: Dated complete-digital-object check for Cambridge University Library MS Dd.5.64.
evidence.summary: Checks of Cambridge Digital Library, ArchiveSearch, iDiscover, CUL catalog guidance, and exact-shelfmark indexes found no publicly obtainable complete Dd.5.64 object. The result is qualified by CUL's statement that most medieval records remain in printed catalogs.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, Yorkshire Writers: Richard Rolle of Hampole and His Followers, vol. 1 (London, 1895).
evidence.summary: Scholarly edition and witness discussion supporting the Dd. v. 64 shelfmark, late-fourteenth-century date, Rolle-associated contents, and the variant-manuscript context used by Day's 1921 Notes.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, Zenodo record 14585961.
evidence.summary: Complete versioned scholarly dataset identifying the prayers, first and last lines, form, subjects, Harley MS 1022 f. 61v loci, and bibliography, under CC BY 4.0.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Duration of Copyright, Circular 15A, revised 2026.
evidence.summary: Current authoritative U.S. guidance stating that works published in the United States before January 1, 1931 are in the public domain, supporting the jurisdiction-limited status of the 1866 and 1921 editions.

open_questions.question: Can Lincoln Cathedral supply a complete digital MS 91 object including covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all front and rear leaves, blanks, fragments, every numbered leaf, edges if offered, and all adjacent works, and under what private-use and redistribution terms?
next_steps: Request a complete-object quotation rather than a CME00058 leaf excerpt.
After receipt, map only ff. 192r-196v, 219v-221v, and 223r-230v as the work_portion while retaining all intervening and surrounding matter.
Record supplied-file color, resolution, completeness, and use terms before caching or reuse.

open_questions.question: Will the British Library approve and quote complete-object color photography of Harley MS 1022 and Royal MS 17 C XVIII, and what exact research-cache, repository, and publication permissions would attach to the supplied files?
next_steps: Submit separate whole-object enquiries naming every exterior and interior surface, blank, flyleaf, pastedown, stub, numbered leaf, and adjacent work.
For Harley identify f. 61v and ff. 62r-64r separately; for Royal identify approximately ff. 57r-64v and ask for the precise Perry-opening crosswalk.
Request color explicitly, obtain an itemized quote, and seek written permission before any repository redistribution or print-on-demand use.

open_questions.question: What is the shelfmark of Perry's unnamed Cambridge Mixed Life witness, and can folio-level inspection confirm the high-confidence inference that Mabel Day's contiguous abbreviated 'MS. Camb.' citations refer to CUL Dd.5.64?
next_steps: Ask CUL Special Collections to identify the comparison sentence printed in Perry's item XI headnote.
Check the printed Dd.5.64 catalog description and manuscript contents against Day's p. 13 and pp. 17-20 variants.
Keep the unidentified Mixed Life witness distinct until the shelfmark is evidenced.

open_questions.question: Which witnesses and editorial interventions directly produced Furnivall's reset 1921 text beyond the demonstrable added Lincoln and Harley items?
next_steps: Compare the 1866 and 1921 texts mechanically and classify substantive versus typographic changes.
Search EETS editorial correspondence and Furnivall papers for the revision files.
Do not treat Day's later Notes citations as proof that every cited witness directly supplied the revised text.

open_questions.question: How should modern scholarship delimit Rolle's authorship among revised items I-IX and Walter Hilton's or anonymous authorship among items X-XIII?
next_steps: Consult modern critical editions and current authorial canons for each individual component.
Retain manuscript rubrics, Perry's historical attributions, and current catalog attributions as separately sourced assertions.
Do not propagate the edition title's single-author wording to the complete selection.

open_questions.question: Will BSB correct the 1921 creation date and associated bibliographic metadata on bsb10748408 to identify the photographed 1866 first edition?
next_steps: Report the internal wrapper, title-page imprint, ten-item numbering, and text/glossary pagination evidence to BSB/MDZ.
Retain the provider's current metadata verbatim in the cached manifest while cataloging the image content as 1866.
